papers.title: The National ALS Biorepository: A CDC-Funded Resource for ALS Research
papers.journal: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis and Frontotemporal Degeneration
papers.notes: 1721 enrolled participants with DNA, blood, serum, and CSF collected longitudinally. CDC-funded with formal public access mechanism. This is the largest and most accessible ALS biorepository in the US with the full trio (CSF+serum+DNA). Access requires CDC application process.

papers.title: AMBRoSIA: A Multi-Site UK ALS Biomarker Study with Longitudinal CSF and Serum Collection
papers.access_route: AMBRoSIA consortium data/sample access request via lead site (Oxford)
papers.notes: 258 ALS cases + 80 controls, serum and CSF collected longitudinally. UK multi-site consortium with Oxford as lead. DNA not listed among collected specimen types — partial trio. Longitudinal design adds value for biomarker trajectory studies. Access via Oxford lead PI.

papers.title: NEALS Sample Repository
papers.notes: Non-paper source. NEALS Sample Repository offers CSF, serum, DNA, and PBMC — the full requested trio plus PBMC. Coordinated through MGH/Harvard. Application process at neals.org. N not publicly specified; inventory varies by specimen type. Strong access infrastructure for academic researchers.

papers.title: Target ALS Biorepository
papers.access_route: Target ALS data/sample access request; targetals.org
papers.notes: 62 ALS cases + 51 controls across 18 US sites. CSF, plasma, and serum collected. DNA not explicitly listed as banked specimen type. Nonprofit foundation with formal access process. Multi-site collection increases generalizability. N is smaller than National Biorepository.

notes: Strong sourcing landscape for ALS CSF+serum+DNA trio. National ALS Biorepository (CDC) is the gold standard with n=1721 and confirmed full trio. NEALS also offers the full trio with formal access. AMBRoSIA is CSF+serum without confirmed DNA — partial trio. Target ALS has CSF+serum but DNA not confirmed. Eval should check: (1) whether each institution has confirmed DNA availability in addition to CSF+serum, and (2) whether AMBRoSIA and Target ALS DNA can be requested separately from stored PBMC.
